title: [ExportVerilog] Wrong bitwidth being printed on module parameters
body:
Given the following IR:

```
hw.module.extern @Cosim_Endpoint(%clk: i1, %rstn: i1, %DataOutReady: i1, %DataInValid: i1, %DataIn: none) -> (%DataOutValid: i1, %DataOut: none, %DataInReady: i1)
hw.module @ints(%clk: i1, %rstn: i1) {
  %DataOutValid, %DataOut, %DataInReady = hw.instance "TestEP" @Cosim_Endpoint(%clk, %rstn, %DataInReady, %DataOutValid, %DataOut) {parameters = {ENDPOINT_ID = 1 : i32, RECV_TYPE_ID = 0578209918096690139 : ui64, RECV_TYPE_SIZE_BITS = 128 : i32, SEND_TYPE_ID = 3057809918096690139 : ui64, SEND_TYPE_SIZE_BITS = 128 : i32}} : (i1, i1, i1, i1, !hw.array<128xi1>) -> (i1, !hw.array<128xi1>, i1)
}
```

ExportVerilog currently produces:
```
// external module Cosim_Endpoint

module ints(
  input clk, rstn);

  wire         TestEP_DataOutValid;     // basic.mlir:3:43
  wire [127:0] TestEP_DataOut;  // basic.mlir:3:43
  wire         TestEP_DataInReady;      // basic.mlir:3:43

  Cosim_Endpoint #(
    .ENDPOINT_ID(1),
    .RECV_TYPE_ID(60'd578209918096690139),
    .RECV_TYPE_SIZE_BITS(128),
    .SEND_TYPE_ID(62'd3057809918096690139),
    .SEND_TYPE_SIZE_BITS(128)
  ) TestEP (    // basic.mlir:3:43
    .clk          (clk),
    .rstn         (rstn),
    .DataOutReady (TestEP_DataInReady), // basic.mlir:3:43
    .DataInValid  (TestEP_DataOutValid),        // basic.mlir:3:43
    .DataIn       (TestEP_DataOut),     // basic.mlir:3:43
    .DataOutValid (TestEP_DataOutValid),
    .DataOut      (TestEP_DataOut),
    .DataInReady  (TestEP_DataInReady)
  );
endmodule
```

The problematic lines being:
```
    .RECV_TYPE_ID(60'd578209918096690139),
    .SEND_TYPE_ID(62'd3057809918096690139),
```

Since the module being [instantiated explicitly requires a 64 bit value](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/8277a62325658b4a17b14f83cb2caa09a78b9ee2/include/circt/Dialect/ESI/cosim/Cosim_Endpoint.sv#L20), Verilator (and presumably other simulators) error out since the widths don't match.

ExportVerilog used to and needs to produce:
```
    .RECV_TYPE_ID(64'd578209918096690139),
    .SEND_TYPE_ID(64'd3057809918096690139),
```

I can boil this down further. This:
```
hw.module.extern @ModuleWithStronglyTypedParameters() -> ()
hw.module @test() {
  hw.instance "TestEP" @ModuleWithStronglyTypedParameters() {parameters = { RECV_TYPE_ID = 00578209918096690139 : ui64}} : () -> ()
}
```
incorrectly produces:
```
// external module ModuleWithStronglyTypedParameters

module test();
  ModuleWithStronglyTypedParameters #(
    .RECV_TYPE_ID(60'd578209918096690139)
  ) TestEP ();  // basic.mlir:3:3
endmodule
```


We should go back to using the type information in the IntegerAttribute (width and signedness) since sometimes Verilog requires that it match rather than relying on implicit metadata from APInt. See https://github.com/llvm/circt/commit/cfd641a71ee721e78234518db2a6f29c3648654b.

@lattner Is there a specific reason that we stopped doing this? I understand the part about not printing the width for values under 32 bits to better accommodate external modules with unknown/unmodeled parameter types.
